response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack web engineer with solid TDD practices and some distributed systems exposure, but he does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The most critical gap is C++: the role requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet C++ does not appear in his skills section, and his only verifiable C/C++ work is approximately 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015. His primary expertise is in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies. His cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) is real but shallow and not at the platform-infrastructure scale the role demands. Additionally, his resume lists a BS from MIT (2009–2012) while simultaneously showing multiple paid positions in Romania and Toronto during the same period, which raises a credibility concern that would need to be addressed before any further consideration.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using low-latency, async I/O techniques
Strong, consistent TDD and software craftsmanship culture evidenced by open-source test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), workshops, and mentoring sessions
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, embedded, and geo-data platforms showing adaptability and problem-solving range
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015) and absent from his listed skills — this is an absolute miss against the 8-year hard requirement
No cloud compute platform ownership or technical leadership of cloud/DevOps engineers; his leadership is informal (workshops, pairing) rather than organizational

Education credentials are internally inconsistent: MIT BS (Cambridge, 2009–2012) overlaps chronologically with simultaneous paid positions in Bucharest, Cluj-Napoca, and Toronto, warranting verification
